title: Peppara Wildlife Sanctuary
tagline: Peppara Wildlife Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Kerala

overview.short: The Peppara Wildlife Sanctuary is a wildlife sanctuary in Thiruvananthapuram district of Kerala, India.
overview.description: The Peppara Wildlife Sanctuary is a wildlife sanctuary in Thiruvananthapuram district of Kerala, India. It consists of the catchment area of the Karamana River, which originates from Chemmunjimottai, the tallest hill within the sanctuary. The sanctuary is named after the Peppara Dam, commissioned in 1983 to augment the drinking water supply to Thiruvananthapuram city and suburban areas. Considering the ecological significance of the area, it was declared a sanctuary in 1983.

topPlaces.name: Vazhvanthol waterfalls
topPlaces.description: Vazhvanthol Waterfalls is a scenic waterfalls located inside Peppara wildlife sanctuary in Thiruvananthapuram district of Kerala state, India. It is situated around 46 km (29 mi) from Thiruvananthapuram on the way to Bonacaud.

topPlaces.name: Peppara Dam
topPlaces.description: Peppara Dam is a masonry gravity dam on the Karamana River in Thiruvananthapuram District. It is built by the Kerala Water Authority in 1983 has a catchment area of 83 km2 and receives an average rain fall of 481 cm.

topPlaces.name: Meenankal
topPlaces.description: Meenankal is a village in Thiruvananthapuram district in the state of Kerala, India, and located around 5 kilometers away from the Peppara Wildlife Sanctuary. Meenankal has border with Uzhamalakkal, Vellanad, Anad, Kuttichal, Vithura, Tholickode panchayaths and Thiruneveli District of Tamil Nadu, India.

topPlaces.name: Vithura
topPlaces.description: Vithura, also known as the Hill City Of Thiruvananthapuram, is located 36 km from Thiruvananthapuram capital of the Kerala state in India. Vithura is the culmination of the crossroads to many tourist, cultural, and religious centres.

topPlaces.name: Memala
topPlaces.description: Memala is a small village in Puramattom panchayat, Pathanamthitta district, Kerala, India. The Peppara dam is near Memala.

topPlaces.name: Aryanadu
topPlaces.description: Aryanadu is a village town in Thiruvananthapuram district, Kerala, India, lying under the foothills of the Agastyarkoodam of the Sahya mountains. The village is spread along the sides of Karamana River in the western ghats of Agasthya Mountains.

topPlaces.name: Agastya Mala
topPlaces.description: Agasthyamala (or Agasthyarkoodam) is one of the peaks of the Agasthyamala Biosphere Reserve in the Western Ghats that lies between the districts of Tirunelveli District of Tamil Nadu and Thiruvananthapuram District of Kerala, India. This peak is specifically located in the Thiruvananthapuram district of Kerala, near the Tamil Nadu border.

Peppara Dam is a masonry gravity dam on the Karamana River in Thiruvananthapuram District. It is built by the Kerala Water Authority in 1983 has a catchment area of 83 km2 and receives an average rain fall of 481 cm.

Meenankal is a village in Thiruvananthapuram district in the state of Kerala, India, and located around 5 kilometers away from the Peppara Wildlife Sanctuary. Meenankal has border with Uzhamalakkal, Vellanad, Anad, Kuttichal, Vithura, Tholickode panchayaths and Thiruneveli District of Tamil Nadu, India.

Vithura, also known as the Hill City Of Thiruvananthapuram, is located 36 km from Thiruvananthapuram capital of the Kerala state in India. Vithura is the culmination of the crossroads to many tourist, cultural, and religious centres.
Memala is a small village in Puramattom panchayat, Pathanamthitta district, Kerala, India. The Peppara dam is near Memala.

Aryanadu is a village town in Thiruvananthapuram district, Kerala, India, lying under the foothills of the Agastyarkoodam of the Sahya mountains. The village is spread along the sides of Karamana River in the western ghats of Agasthya Mountains.

Agasthyamala (or Agasthyarkoodam) is one of the peaks of the Agasthyamala Biosphere Reserve in the Western Ghats that lies between the districts of Tirunelveli District of Tamil Nadu and Thiruvananthapuram District of Kerala, India. This peak is specifically located in the Thiruvananthapuram district of Kerala, near the Tamil Nadu border.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Peppara Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Peppara Wildlife Sanctuary is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Peppara Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Thiruvananthapuram International Airport (33 km) and the nearest railway station is Thiruvananthapuram Central (30 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Peppara Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Peppara Wildlife Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Peppara Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Peppara Wildlife Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Peppara Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The top attractions are Vazhvanthol waterfalls, Peppara Dam, Meenankal, Vithura, Memala.
